odd_case.uniqueness: a is unique by maximality in the definition of v2

proof_payload.theorem: forall n > 1 exists k >= 1 such that C^k(n) < n

statement: For every positive integer n > 1, even n descends immediately and odd n enters a unique parent state n = 2^a q - 1.
Every parent-state domain required by the theorem is covered by replayed coverage, residual parent, finite/base, or no-escape certificates.
Every transition used by the theorem graph is an exact replayed Collatz/parent-state/debt transition.
The closed parent-state transition system has no infinite non-descending path by explicit well-founded ranking.

proof_payload.collatz_conjecture: forall n > 1 exists t >= 0 such that C^t(n) = 1
proof_payload.descent_theorem: forall n > 1 exists k >= 1 such that C^k(n) < n

strong_induction.composition_step: append the descent trajectory from n to m to the induction trajectory from m to 1
strong_induction.descent_step: top-level descent gives k >= 1 and m = C^k(n) with 1 <= m < n
strong_induction.induction_hypothesis: for all 1 <= m < n, m reaches 1
strong_induction.positivity_preservation: C maps positive integers to positive integers, so m >= 1

statement: The replayed top-level system proves Collatz descent, and descent implies convergence to 1 by strong induction.
